compiled_state.title: Java First-Class Language Support: full stack detection, routing, and scaffolding
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Context
Java is entirely unsupported: detect-stack.sh has no Java detection, Java projects fall through to unknown routing and receive no test/lint/format gates, no kudos triggers, no security scan, and no CI skeleton support. This epic adds Java as a first-class language: detect-stack.sh detection (Maven/Gradle with wrapper preference), onboarding Java branch, commands.* routing, gate-2d/gate-2b Java support, project-detect.sh CI pattern recognition, plus stretch deliverables (commands.security OWASP scan and architect-foundation scaffolding).

Core tier (sprint-blocking): SCs 1-3, 6, 7. Stretch (not required for epic closure): SCs 4, 5.

## Success Criteria
- detect-stack.sh adds java-maven (pom.xml at root) and java-gradle (build.gradle at root) as separate stack values; CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md stack accepted values list updated to include both; java-maven takes priority when both present; Java detection below Python/Rust/Go/Node/Ruby; scripts treating both as equivalent (gate-2b, gate-2d, project-detect) vs. differently (commands.* writes, wrapper detection) documented via equivalence contract
- onboarding SKILL.md Phase 3 Step 2b updated with Java branch writing commands.test_runner, commands.lint, commands.format, commands.format_check; wrapper detection (root-only): mvnw present -> ./mvnw test; pom.xml without mvnw -> mvn test; gradlew present -> ./gradlew test; build.gradle without gradlew -> gradle test
- commands.lint = mvn checkstyle:check, commands.format_check = mvn spotless:check, commands.format = mvn spotless:apply for Java stacks
- [STRETCH] commands.security config key added to CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md; validate.sh emits [DSO WARN] commands.security not configured with exit 0 when absent; Java defaults: mvn org.owasp:dependency-check-maven:check -DfailBuildOnCVSS=7 (Maven), ./gradlew dependencyCheckAnalyze (Gradle)
- [STRETCH] architect-foundation Phase 3 Enforcer Setup: checkstyle.xml using Google Java Style, maven-checkstyle-plugin v3.3.1 + spotless-maven-plugin v2.43.0 in pom.xml with executions bound to validate phase; mvn validate passes after scaffolding
- gate-2d-dependency-check.sh adds Java import statement extraction; gate-2b-blast-radius.sh adds Java fan-in patterns (import, class instantiation) as bash array elements (not eval)
- project-detect.sh recognizes mvn test and ./gradlew test patterns in GitHub Actions YAML
- Validation: grep commands.test_runner .claude/dso-config.conf returns mvn test after onboarding Maven project; grep 'java-maven|java-gradle' CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md returns both entries

## Dependencies
- Epic A (87e9-0bc8): commands.test_runner routing must exist before Epic F writes Maven/Gradle defaults; onboarding config-write path must be established
- Epic B (3818-310d): commands.lint, commands.format, commands.format_check must exist; gate-2d/gate-2b structure lands first (SC6 is additive Java-only entries)
- Epic C (co-deployment only, not blocking): once both Epic C and Epic F merge, T7/T13/T4 automatically support Java via lizard/jscpd
- Epic E (not blocking): SC5 is self-contained architect-foundation Phase 3 addition; no Epic E deliverable required for any Epic F story

## Approach
Add Java detection to detect-stack.sh (java-maven and java-gradle stack values). Update onboarding SKILL.md with a Java branch in the config-write phase. Add Java defaults to the commands.* routing table. Extend gate-2d and gate-2b with Java patterns. Add project-detect.sh CI pattern recognition. Stretch: add commands.security and architect-foundation scaffolding templates.
